commit.message:
Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

fix: revert dir=rtl on BattleScreen root — broke knight/enemy positions and Hebrew ! placement

The battle layout uses absolute positioning that assumes LTR. Only the score
spans need dir=ltr to prevent number+label bidi reordering, not the root.

commit.message:
Send download link instead of preview after resume generation

Merge processGeneratePreview and processDownload into single
processGenerateResume that extracts data, generates DOCX, and
sends a short message with the download URL.

Reply options: 1 = edit resume, 2 = create another resume.
"1"/"2" handled contextually when resume data exists.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add batch mode rebase-and-rebuild for merge conflicts

When parallel batch tasks produce merge conflicts, automatically retry
by creating fresh worktrees from the integration branch tip, copying
reusable artifacts (spec, plan, TDD tests), and re-running only
build+codereview+verify phases. Adds --max-rebase-rounds, --no-rebase,
and --merge-strategy flags with config file support.

  there's not enough space below (both apps)
- Touch targets: header buttons increased from 40x40 to 44x44px
- Compare card padding: reduced from 48px to 36px on mobile for
  tighter scrolling experience
- oscar_best_director: fixed literal "Best Director" string in all
  75 data files with correct winning film names, including 26 years
  where Best Director and Best Picture diverged

pipeline registration, and golden file tests. Sub-tasks reference

marked as implemented. Added RULE-008 (generator integration).

commit.message:
fix: display name not showing due to async profile loading

Added fallback profile loading from localStorage to handle race condition where user session loads before profile data. The header now uses effectiveProfile (authProfile || fallbackProfile) to immediately display user info while the full profile loads asynchronously. Also added comprehensive debug logging to track profile loading state.

commit.message:
Initial commit: schema, taxonomy, methodology, prototype site

CAIHO (Canadian AI Hazard Observatory) — public-interest documentation
of AI incidents and hazards with a Canada nexus.

Includes:
- Record schema (JSON Schema) with two-layer taxonomy (native + OECD interop)
- Verification ladder (verification_level + dispute_status)
- Taxonomy with 18 domains, 14 harm types, 12 AI involvement mechanisms
- OECD AIM and AIID crosswalk documentation
- PostgreSQL database schema
- Astro SSG bilingual prototype site (EN/FR) with 10 sample records
- Read-only API specification

commit.message:
feat: réactiver budget dispatch Bot Z → sub-bots (paper trading complet)

Le dispatch était désactivé temporairement après un bug de corruption.
La cause racine est maintenant corrigée (sanity cap dans bot_z.py :
weighted_return > 15% → recalage sur ratio réel des bot_values).

Améliorations vs ancienne implémentation :
- Save immédiate des states scalés après dispatch (persistance si crash)
- Notification Telegram uniquement si changement > 15% ET après 1er cycle
  (évite le spam au démarrage)
- States sont cleanés (1000€, no positions) → premier dispatch cohérent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(deploy): include .prisma/client in standalone — fixes ContainerTimeout

Root cause of "Starting the site..." → ContainerTimeout crash loop:

  @prisma/client requires .prisma/client at IMPORT TIME (synchronous).
  Next.js standalone @vercel/nft tracer never includes .prisma/client
  because it is a GENERATED artifact (from prisma generate), not an
  installed package — so nft's file trace misses it entirely.

  At runtime: server.js imports prisma.ts → import PrismaClient from
  '@prisma/client' → @prisma/client/index.js tries to load
  .prisma/client/index.js → MODULE_NOT_FOUND → uncaught exception →
  process exits before listen() is ever called → Azure warmup probe
  receives no response → ContainerTimeout after 230s.

Fix: explicitly copy .prisma/client from the pnpm store into
standalone/node_modules/.prisma/client before packaging, alongside
the existing styled-jsx patch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(sse): decouple LLM generation from HTTP connection for reconnection

Previously, runtime.query() ran inline in the SSE generator, so client
disconnect (CancelledError) killed both the HTTP response and the LLM
generation. Reconnecting clients would hang forever because the buffer
had no producer and was never marked done.

Now runtime.query() runs as a background asyncio.create_task that
survives client disconnect. The SSE endpoint only subscribes to the
EventBuffer. Added EventBuffer.mark_done() to signal completion without
a Done event (e.g. on unexpected errors).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix populate_training_week failures: validation, tool rounds, and instruction enforcement

Schema: allow reps as string or integer — Bill consistently sends strings,
causing validation loops that exhaust tool rounds and timeout the request.

Validator: add hard errors for:
- Main strength step missing pattern_type (STRENGTH_FLAT etc.)
- Main strength step missing rest_seconds
- Sessions >= 20 min with no cooldown segment
These silent omissions were passing through and writing thin data to Sheets.

claude_client: increase max_tool_rounds 10 -> 20 to support multi-session
population with proper exercise lookup.

Instructions: explicit per-step checklists (strength vs cardio/running),
batch exercise lookup workflow (use exercise_filter first, not search_exercises
per exercise), cardio/running must use duration/interval fields not reps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
major balance overhaul — harder, more strategic, both roles matter

- Enemy scaling: 7+turn*6 ±10 (was 8+turn*5 ±3) — harder, unpredictable
- Enemy types: cavalry (walls useless), siege (walls half, destroys building on loss),
  ambush (troop power halved), standard — shown to players each turn so they can plan
- Wall effectiveness decays vs stronger enemies (max 0.3 floor) — no pure wall stacking
- Building destroyed on any loss — Defence minister protects Finance's work
- City prerequisites: requires market, max 3 — kills the city-spam loop
- Starting state: 100 gold, farm only, infantry:3 + militia:5 — no free head-start
- Battle modal shows enemy type, wall effectiveness modifier, destroyed building

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

logs to billing, OpenAI Usage API added, Gemini free tier note.

placeholders on Level 11 Events. Performance 78→80.

commit.message:
Add TRT acceleration pipeline (Phase 1-3)

Phase 1 - BEV Encoder TRT:
- plugins/: MSDAPlugin, RotatePlugin, InversePlugin (cuBLAS batched matrix inverse)
- functions/: Python ONNX-export wrappers for custom ops
- encoder.py, spatial_cross_attention.py, temporal_self_attention.py, transformer.py:
  TRT-compatible module variants

Phase 2 - Detection Heads TRT:
- modules/decoder.py: CustomMSDeformableAttentionTRT, DetectionTransformerDecoderTRTP
- dense_heads/track_head.py: BEVFormerTrackHeadTRT.get_detections_trt()
- configs: univ2x_coop_e2e_track_trt_p2.py

Phase 3 - Downstream Heads TRT (Motion + Occ + Planning):
- tools/export_onnx_univ2x.py: BEVEncoderWrapper, HeadsWrapper, DownstreamHeadsWrapper
  with onnx_compatible_attention() (SDPA/unflatten/dropout/BN-freeze patches)
- tools/build_trt_downstream.py: standalone TRT engine builder
- tools/validate_downstream_trt.py: Step A/B validation

Step B accuracy (200x200, stg2 checkpoint): cosine > 0.9999854 on all outputs

Adds the interactions project scaffolding (templates, scripts, catalog)
and the first interaction — a signature writer that converts typed names
into SVG paths via opentype.js and animates them with a write-on effect.
